ksa_text: Design enterprise information architecture and systems to support organizational objectives, including alignment of data architecture with business strategy and capability delivery.

dcwf.top_element_label: Develop a data management strategy that helps to prioritize investments and resource allocations (e.g., data analytics, data infrastructure).

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Develop a data management strategy that helps to prioritize investments and resource allocations (e.g., data analytics, data infrastructure).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although Data Governance is an edge domain for the Director of AI/ML, this task KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.463) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.463). External reviewers may question whether this specific task KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of AI/ML versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK, EDM Council, and enterprise CDO job descriptions universally include data governance competencies for data leadership roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Data Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of AI/ML.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain Data Governance task KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.463. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the Director of AI/ML.
